steps.title: Register on NVSP Portal
steps.description: You need to register as a voter first. Visit the National Voter Service Portal to start your application.
steps.how_to: Go to https://voters.eci.gov.in → Click 'New Voter Registration' → Select Form 6 → Fill your details (name, age, address, constituency).
steps.tip: You can also register offline at your nearest ERO (Electoral Registration Officer) office.

steps.title: Track Your Application
steps.description: After submitting Form 6, track your application status online until your Voter ID is approved.
steps.how_to: Use your reference number on the NVSP portal or the Voter Helpline App (available on Android & iOS) to check status.
steps.tip: Processing usually takes 2-4 weeks. If rejected, you can re-apply with corrected documents.

steps.title: Collect Your Voter ID (EPIC)
steps.description: Once approved, collect your EPIC (Election Photo Identity Card) from your local ERO office or download the e-EPIC.
steps.how_to: Visit your nearest ERO office with the approval letter, OR download your e-EPIC from https://voters.eci.gov.in (Digital Voter ID).
steps.tip: The e-EPIC (digital Voter ID) is equally valid and can be stored on your phone.

steps.title: Check Your Name in the Electoral Roll
steps.description: Before election day, verify that your name appears in the voter list for your constituency.
steps.how_to: Visit https://electoralsearch.eci.gov.in and search by name or EPIC number.
steps.tip: Do this at least 2 weeks before election day to resolve any issues.

steps.title: Find Your Polling Booth
steps.description: Locate your assigned polling station. You can only vote at the booth assigned to your address.
steps.how_to: Your polling booth is mentioned on your voter slip or you can find it on the Voter Helpline App.
documents_needed: Voter Slip or Voter Helpline App
steps.tip: Visit the booth a day before to familiarize yourself with the location.

steps.title: Carry Valid Photo ID
steps.description: On election day, carry your EPIC (Voter ID card) or any of the 12 approved photo identity documents.
steps.how_to: Accepted IDs include: Aadhaar, Passport, Driving License, PAN Card, and others specified by ECI.
documents_needed: EPIC or any 1 of 12 approved photo IDs
steps.tip: Keep a photocopy as backup. If you've lost your EPIC, other government photo IDs will work.

steps.title: Go to Your Polling Booth
steps.description: Voting hours are typically 7:00 AM to 6:00 PM. Visit during off-peak hours (mid-morning or afternoon) to avoid long queues.
steps.how_to: Arrive at your assigned booth. Join the queue. Maintain order and follow instructions from polling officers.
steps.tip: If you're in queue before 6:00 PM, you will be allowed to vote even if it takes longer.

steps.title: Verification & Inking
steps.description: The polling officer will verify your identity, check your name in the register, and apply indelible ink to your left index finger.
steps.how_to: Show your ID, sign or provide thumb impression in the register. The ink mark prevents duplicate voting.
steps.tip: The ink is harmless and wears off naturally in 2-3 weeks.

steps.title: Cast Your Vote on the EVM
steps.description: Enter the voting compartment alone. Press the button next to your chosen candidate on the Electronic Voting Machine (EVM).
steps.how_to: The EVM will show candidate names and party symbols. Press the blue button next to your choice. A beep confirms your vote. The VVPAT slip will be visible for 7 seconds for verification.
steps.tip: Your vote is secret. No one can see who you voted for. If you don't want to vote for anyone, you can press the NOTA button.

steps.title: Exit the Booth
steps.description: After casting your vote, leave the polling booth quietly. Do not discuss your vote inside the booth area.
steps.how_to: Exit through the designated gate. Your duty as a voter is complete!
steps.tip: Many employers grant paid leave on election day. Check with your company.

underage_step.title: You're Not Old Enough Yet
underage_step.description: You must be at least 18 years old on the qualifying date (January 1 of the year of revision of the electoral roll) to vote in India.
underage_step.how_to: You can pre-register up to 3 months before turning 18. Visit https://voters.eci.gov.in and fill Form 6 with your upcoming 18th birthday date.
documents_needed: Age Proof (Birth Certificate or School Certificate)
underage_step.tip: Start learning about the election process now so you're ready when the time comes!
